supporting_quotes.quote: the core genome had fewer NEG and VEG than the flexible genome (1.5% < 6.6% and 49.6% < 64.6%, respectively; P < 0.001; Figure 3c)

HEG.name: Prochlorococcus MED4 cluster HEG (highly expressed)
HEG.functional_description: Enriched for genes involved in energy production and conversion, translation and ribosomal structure, and protein modification, folding and turnover. Includes ribosomal proteins and photosynthetic apparatus genes.
HEG.temporal_pattern: Genes in this cluster are consistently highly expressed across multiple growth conditions.
HEG.enrichment_category: Energy production and conversion; Translation; Protein folding and turnover

supporting_quotes.quote: Among these core HEG genes, several functional categories were more prominent than others. These included the “C” (energy production and conversion), “J” (translation and ribosomal structure), and “O” (protein modification, folding and turnover) categories (Figure 4c).
HEG had a significantly lower nonsynonymous substitution rate (Ka) than MEG or LEG (Kruskal-Wallis Test, two-tailed P < 0.001; Figure 3a)

LEG.name: Prochlorococcus MED4 cluster LEG (lowly expressed)

supporting_quotes.quote: Additionally, category “R” (general function) was slightly enriched in both LEG and NEG (P = 0.023 and 0.055; data not shown).

LEG.assessment_notes: Only slight enrichment reported for general function category.

MEG.name: Prochlorococcus MED4 cluster MEG (moderately expressed)
MEG.functional_description: Enriched for essential genes with homologs in the Database of Essential Genes (DEG).
MEG.temporal_pattern: Genes in this cluster show moderate expression levels across conditions.

supporting_quotes.quote: Although the MEG subclass had a significantly higher rate of DEG-hit genes (P < 0.001; Figure 4b)
the core genome had fewer NEG and VEG than the flexible genome (1.5% < 6.6% and 49.6% < 64.6%, respectively; P < 0.001; Figure 3c)
